description: Phase 6 — Semantic Density: dense compound P_p vs. diluted P_p on invisible timing failure (calibration fix: charge inside transaction)
notes: Phase 6 semantic density hypothesis. Calibration fix from exp-10: payment.charge() moved inside db.transaction() — charge-before-commit race eliminated, no visible pointer to PaymentClient. Only non-surface finding: ORDER_LOCK_TTL=60 < worst-case payment.charge() of 100s (3 retries × 30s + 2 × 5s backoff). A (dense P_p): fused compound identity with compulsion language. B (diluted P_p): same semantic content in 5-6 separate unfused statements, no compulsion language. C (P_d baseline). Primary question: A > B on detection rate? Secondary: B > C?
